title: Chapter 6 — Skills: Build Once, Use Every Semester
CHAPTER 6 —
SKILLS: BUILD ONCE, USE EVERY SEMESTER
segments.body_html:
This chapter gives a working overview of Skills: Build Once, Use Every Semester, focusing on the ideas a reader needs be

Chapter 6 — Skills: Build Once, Use Ever — A Skill is a CLAUDE.md file for a specific task — a lesson-plan generator, a rubric builder, a grading workflow. Build i

beats.text: This chapter gives a working overview of Skills: Build Once, Use Every Semester, focusing on the ideas a reader needs before moving to the next chapter. The chapter moves through Learning outcomes, Opening, What a Skill is, Workflow, and related ideas. Read it for the main argument, the vocabulary it introduces, and the practical judgment it asks you to develop. A Skill is a CLAUDE.md file for a specific task , a lesson-plan generator, a rubric builder, a grading workflow. Build it once.

segments.label: 01 / 13 · HOOK

title: OPENING
segments.body_html:
The teacher had written the same grading-workflow specification for the third time.

Opening — Different assignment. Different week. Same workflow: read submissions, check for required sections, flag patterns, outpu

beats.text: The teacher had written the same grading-workflow specification for the third time. Different assignment. Different week. Same workflow: read submissions, check for required sections, flag patterns, output per-student flags , never generate a final grade, never write directly to the student. The third time, the spec was 280 words long and took 18 minutes to compose. Claude executed cleanly. The drafts came out right.

segments.label: 02 / 13 · OPENING

title: SKILLS VS. CLAUDE.MD:
THE BOUNDARY
segments.body_html:
Both are markdown files Claude reads. The difference is when Claude reads them.

Skills vs. CLAUDE.md: the boundary — CLAUDE.md loads at session start. Every prompt in the session operates with CLAUDE.md in context. Use CLAUDE.md for what

beats.text: Both are markdown files Claude reads. The difference is when Claude reads them. CLAUDE.md loads at session start. Every prompt in the session operates with CLAUDE.md in context. Use CLAUDE.md for what must be true across all work in this project: the project's stack, code style deviations, environment quirks, never-rules.

segments.label: 03 / 13 · SKILLS VS. CLAUDE.MD: THE BOUN

title: THE TWO
INVOCATION MODES.
segments.body_html:
Skills can be invoked two ways. The flag in the frontmatter controls which.

The two invocation modes — disable-model-invocation: false (default). Claude can decide to invoke the skill automatically when it judges the skill

beats.text: Skills can be invoked two ways. The flag in the frontmatter controls which. disable-model-invocation: false (default). Claude can decide to invoke the skill automatically when it judges the skill is relevant to the current prompt. Useful for skills that are uncontroversial when invoked , a lesson-plan generator, a rubric builder. The risk: Claude invokes the skill when you did not intend.

segments.label: 04 / 13 · THE TWO INVOCATION MODES

title: THE TEACHER
SKILL CATALOG.
segments.body_html:
lesson-plan-generator — Produce a draft lesson plan from learning objectives
rubric-builder — Generate a draft rubric from an assignment description
grading-workflow — Flag patterns across submissions; produce per-student flag reports
differentiation-helper — Suggest differentiation moves for a lesson plan

beats.text: The skills most teachers will want by the end of one semester: The three with disable-model-invocation: true are the ones with side effects on files , they should run only when you explicitly invoke them. The rest are content-generation that Claude can auto-invoke without surprise.

segments.label: 05 / 13 · THE TEACHER SKILL CATALOG

title: NEVER
segments.body_html:
·Generate content for a topic without an explicit objective input.
·Use generic activities ("students will discuss"); name the specific protocol.
·Omit the differentiation section.

beats.text: Generate content for a topic without an explicit objective input. Use generic activities ("students will discuss"); name the specific protocol. Omit the differentiation section. Step 4: commit. git add .claude/skills/lesson-plan-generator/SKILL.md and and git commit -m "Skill: lesson-plan-generator". The skill is now part of the project's source.

title: SKILLS VS. HOOKS —
PREVIEW
segments.body_html:
Hooks are Chapter 7's full topic. The preview here:

Skills vs. Hooks — preview — A Skill's constraints (the Never section above) are advisory — Claude reads them and tries to follow them. The complianc

beats.text: Hooks are Chapter 7's full topic. The preview here: A Skill's constraints (the Never section above) are advisory , Claude reads them and tries to follow them. The compliance is high but not absolute. If a Skill says "Never generate a final grade" and you tell Claude to generate a final grade, Claude may comply with your instruction even though the Skill prohibits it.

Classroom activity: students build a ski — They write the task as a SKILL.md:

beats.text: Each student identifies a task they do repeatedly in a project they are working on , generating a test case structure, formatting a data analysis report, scaffolding a new feature. Repeated work in any direction. Frontmatter (name, description, invocation mode). Body with workflow steps, constraints, never-rules, verification.

segments.label: 09 / 13 · CLASSROOM ACTIVITY: STUDENTS B

title: COMMON MISCONCEPTIONS
segments.body_html: "Skills are just longer prompts."→"Everything that's reusable should be a Skill."→"Skills are for power users."→"The first Skill I write will be good."→"Skills compete with CLAUDE.md."

beats.text: "Skills are just longer prompts." No. Skills are stored , invokable by name , reusable across sessions and across semesters. "Everything that's reusable should be a Skill." No. The CLAUDE.md / Skill boundary matters. Every-session content is CLAUDE.md; workflow-specific content is a Skill.

segments.label: 10 / 13 · COMMON MISCONCEPTIONS

title: AI WAYBACK
MACHINE.
segments.body_html:
Charles Babbage (1791–1871) — British mathematician whose designs for the Analytical Engine (1837–1871) included the con

AI Wayback Machine — A Claude Code Skill is a Babbage subroutine in 2026 form. The teacher writes the grading workflow once. The workflow is

beats.text: Charles Babbage (1791, 1871) , British mathematician whose designs for the Analytical Engine (1837, 1871) included the conceptual foundation for what would later be called the subroutine: a stored, named operation that could be invoked at any point in a program's execution without being re-specified each time. Babbage's argument, articulated in his autobiography Passages from the Life of a Philosopher (1864), was that the Engine's power came not from any individual operation but from the reusability of operations across many computations.
